En: Maybe it's possible to... talk to it?
Zh: 也许可以……跟它说话？

En: Officer, what are you looking at? He follows your gaze, attempting to see whatever it is that you are seeing.
Zh: “警官，上面有什么东西吗？”警督追随者你的目光，试着辨认你看到的东西。

En: It's like you see your own face reflected back at you in the vanishing geometry -- a strange, alien image that's supposed to be you.
Zh: 就好像在消失的几何图形中看见你自己反射出来的脸庞——本应是你的一种奇怪又陌生的图像。

En: Maybe it's possible to... talk to it?
Zh: 也许可以……跟它说话？

En: But to what? There is almost nothing.
Zh: 但是，跟什么说话呢？那里几乎什么也没有啊。

En: Can't hear shit.
Zh: “什么狗屁都听不见。”

En: Relax. It'll be okay.
Zh: 放松，会没事的。

En: It's *climbing*. Climbing down, holding on the beams.
Zh: 它正在*攀爬*。爬下来，抓在横梁上。

En: It's probably nothing, just our imagination.
Zh: “可能什么也不是，只是我们的想象。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't reply, but you can sense him tense up next to you.
Zh: 警督没有回应，但你能感到身边的他变得有些紧张。

En: He doesn't seem entirely convinced, though.
Zh: 不过他好像没有完全信服。

En: Not really, but it's extraordinary. I've never experienced anything like this.
Zh: “不算吧，不过真的很特别。我从来没有过这种经历。”

En: I wonder why the... church was built with such strange acoustics...
Zh: “不知道为什么……教堂要搞出这么奇怪的音响效果……”

En: His detached tone conceals how uncomfortable he is.
Zh: 他用冷漠的语气掩饰着自己的不安。

En: Maybe the church was designed in this way to prevent boisterous activity -- singing and dancing -- on its premises?
Zh: 也许教堂这么设计是为了防止喧闹的活动——不让别人在这个地盘上载歌载舞？

En: Maybe they wanted to discourage singing and dancing.
Zh: “也许他们不想别人在这里载歌载舞。”

En: Hey, what if it's something *paranatural*?
Zh: “喂，我说，如果是*超自然现象*会怎么样？”

En: Whatever it is, it's definitely real. Something odd is happening around us.
Zh: “不管是什么，它肯定是真实的。我们周围确实发生了很奇怪的事情。”

En: Hmm, could be...
Zh: “嗯，也许吧……”

En: Please, detective, not this again.
Zh: “拜托，警探，别又来这一套。”

En: He's trying to act dismissive, but at this particular moment, he's more open than usual to the idea of paranatural phenomena.
Zh: “拜托，警探，别又来这一套。”

En: Maybe if you were to stand in just the right spot, even your footsteps would be completely silent...
Zh: 如果站在正确的点上，说不定连你的脚步声都会完全消失不见……

En: It seems that sound here is detached from its source somehow, if not blotted out outright. Truly unusual.
Zh: 看来这里的声音以某种方法挣脱了它的源头，如果没有被彻底清除的话。真的很不寻常。

En: Sounds like the pale.
Zh: 听起来很像灰域。

En: It's unnerving.
Zh: 让人感觉很不安。

En: How do I know what the pale sounds like?
Zh: 我怎么知道灰域是什么声音？

En: No. Let me just listen.
Zh: 不。让我听听。

En: From recordings. Of the far pale. You've heard them. We all have.
Zh: 从录音里。遥远的灰域。你听过它们的声音。我们全都听过。

En: You can hardly hear your own breathing.
Zh: 你几乎听不见自己的呼吸声了。

En: You know, in the middle of the last century, there was a group of Revacholian painters who famously disdained the use of black.
Zh: 你知道吗，在上世纪中叶，有一群瑞瓦肖的画家就是因为鄙视使用黑色闻名的。

En: What did they have against black?
Zh: 他们对黑色有什么意见吗？

En: Why only Revacholian?
Zh: 为什么只有瑞瓦肖人？

En: Can I use this to see through the darkness up there, somehow? (Proceed.)
Zh: 这些信息能帮我看穿周围的黑暗吗？（继续。）

En: No, this is just an intellectual musing. In fact, it's probably dangerous to waste time like this. You should stop.
Zh: 不，这只是一种智力的冥想。事实上，这样浪费时间可能是很危险的。你应该停下来。

En: Revachol was an overwhelmingly colourful place back then. They made most of the world's dyes here.
Zh: 那个时候的瑞瓦肖是一个极为色彩艳丽的地方。世界上绝大多数染料都是在这里生产的。

En: They wanted to capture what it really felt like to look at the world -- to be *inside* colour, to be blinded by early afternoon light glittering on the crests of sea waves.
Zh: 他们真的很想捕捉到看着这个世界的真实感受——*深入*色彩内部，为午后浪尖的闪光而盲目。

En: Such an effect is best achieved by using the juxtaposition of complementary colours -- rather than the addition of black -- to create contrast.
Zh: 这种效果最好是使用并置的互补色实现——而不是增加黑色——来创造对比。

En: Hmm, maybe I should take up painting.
Zh: 嗯，也许我应该开始画画。

En: Seems unfair to black. I like black, look at it!
Zh: 好像对黑色不太公平。我喜欢黑色，看看它！

En: It's unclear if you have enough years left in you to make up for your lack of technique...
Zh: 不清楚你余下的人生是否足够填补技术的鸿沟……

En: Sometimes an encounter with an opposing aesthetic is required for one to truly grow into one's tastes and preferences.
Zh: 有时候，遇到一种对立的审美理念是真正提升自我品味和偏好的必要条件。

En: It would likely be easier to explain with a hands-on demonstration. Alas!
Zh: 要是有亲身实践的案例，那解释起来就容易多了。哎！

En: Sounds complicated.
Zh: 听起来很复杂。

En: Prepare for an attack!
Zh: 准备迎战！

En: Officer, is there something up there? The lieutenant follows your gaze, attempting to see whatever it is that you are seeing.
Zh: “警官，上面有什么东西吗？”警督追随者你的目光，试着辨认你看到的东西。

En: The silence in this part of the church -- it's almost palpable. All the shifting matter and shuffling of living things is gone. Nothing seems to exist beyond the church anymore.
Zh: 教堂里这个部分的寂静——几乎是可以感知的。所有不断变化的物质和活物的动静——全都消失不见。似乎除了教堂之外，一切都不复存在。

En: Wait, I think I still hear something...
Zh: 等等，我好像还是听到了什么……

En: And then it's gone. Almost all of it -- but for the faintest of hums.
Zh: 然后又没了。基本上都没了——只剩下最微弱的嗡嗡声。

En: Yell as loud as you can.
Zh: 用尽全力大喊。

En: (Turn to Kim.) "What's happening?"
Zh: （转向金。）“出什么事了？”

En: Look up into the bell tower.
Zh: 抬头仰望钟楼。

En: The orderly rows of ceiling panels become barely visible, then disappear completely in the darkness of the tower overhead.
Zh: 顶上那排整整齐齐的天花板本来就很难辨认，不久便完全消失在塔楼上方那漆黑的阴影之中。

En: It's like there's something moving up there -- a shadow has emerged from the tower and is making its way *toward* you through all the other shadows.
Zh: 上面好像有什么东西在动——塔楼上冒出一个阴影，它正穿越重重阴影*朝着*你的方向移了过来。

En: Try to see beyond the shadows.
Zh: 试着看穿阴影。

En: Is someone watching?
Zh: 有人在看你吗？

En: Just darkness without end -- it makes your head spin.
Zh: 只有无边无际的黑暗——让你的脑袋天旋地转。

En: (Blink.)
Zh: （眨眼。）

En: You see something hanging from the rafters, looking straight at you with dark eyes.
Zh: 你看见有什么东西挂在房椽上，用漆黑的眼睛直直地盯着你。

En: It's not a shadow anymore -- becoming more substantial as it gets closer. The shape of an *animal* descends.
Zh: 它不再是一个阴影——越来越靠近的它变得更像个实体。一个*动物*的形状落了下来。

En: Oh no! You've lost sight of it. Where did it go?
Zh: 不！你看不见它了。到底去哪了？

En: The lieutenant points to his ears and shakes his head. Then he leans closer.
Zh: 警督指了指自己的耳朵，摇了摇头。随后又靠近了一点。

En: Your voice is barely audible -- not a howl, but the softest of whimpers.
Zh: 几乎无法听见你的声音——不是一声嚎叫，而是最轻柔的低语。

En: Stomp your feet and clap your hands.
Zh: 跺跺脚，拍拍手。

En: You produce a few muffled thumps, after which the silence feels even more... *total* somehow.
Zh: 你制造出一些沉闷的声响，但是不知怎么地，随之而来的寂静甚至变得更加……*完整*了。

En: Can you hear anything?
Zh: “你听见什么了吗？”

En: Almost nothing, and it's beginning to worry me.
Zh: “几乎什么也听不见，我有些担心了。”

En: The church just has... strange acoustics -- some engineering trick.
Zh: “这座教堂有一种……奇怪的音响效果——某种工程学上的技巧。”

En: On the ceiling?
Zh: 在天花板上？

En: Follow the shadow's movements.
Zh: 追踪阴影的行动。

En: Yes. The darkness makes the ceiling feel infinitely far away...
Zh: 是的。黑暗让天花板变得似乎遥不可及……

En: Try to make out something -- anything.
Zh: 尝试辨认出什么——任何东西都可以。

En: There's nothing. You're dizzy and disoriented as you see dark and more dark -- rising...
Zh: 什么也没有。你看着那片黑暗，感到一阵眩晕和迷失，而且还有更多黑暗——正在升起……

En: What's the darkness like?
Zh: 黑暗是什么样的？

En: Filled with vague shapes of woodwork. The sense of a great height.
Zh: 满是木制品的模糊阴影。很高的感觉。

En: You see something hanging from the rafters, looking straight at you with dark eyes.
Zh: 你看见有什么东西挂在房椽上，用漆黑的眼睛直直地盯着你。

En: (Blink.)
Zh: （眨眼。）

En: What if I don't *want* to know what's up there?
Zh: 如果我*不想*知道上面有什么呢？

En: The silence, the darkness -- they've enveloped you, as in a cocoon. You cannot move anymore.
Zh: 寂静，黑暗——编织成一个黑茧将你笼罩。你再也动不了了。

En: Look up into the bell tower.
Zh: 抬头仰望钟楼。

En: Hmm, maybe. Darkness often has that effect on people.
Zh: “嗯，也许吧。黑暗经常会对人造成影响。”

En: They really have an interesting set up here. Like kids preparing for a school science fair. It's pretty neat.
Zh: 他们这里布置的确实很有意思。就像孩子们在准备学校的科学展览，非常整洁。

En: Are we sure about this? Something smells fishy...
Zh: 你确定吗？感觉好像有些可疑……

En: Hey Andre, what's all this? (Continue.)
Zh: “我说，安德烈，这是在干什么？”（继续。）

En: This? Oh, we're trying to see if we can come up with some cool new special effects to add to the visual aspect of our club experience... He gestures toward the set-up proudly.
Zh: “这个？哦，我们想看看能不能想出什么酷炫的新效果，给俱乐部增加点视觉体验……”他骄傲地朝装饰品比了个手势。

En: We tinker with things. Art, music... chemistry.
Zh: “我们喜欢摆弄东西。艺术啊，音乐啊……还有化学。”

En: Some chemicals when mixed can, you know, change colour, or sort of -- blow up...
Zh: “你也知道，有些化学品混合的时候可以变色，或者是——爆炸……”

En: BLOW UP! BLOW UP! The young man with the large head pumps his fist in the air.
Zh: “爆炸！爆炸！”那个大头年轻人朝空中挥舞着拳头。

En: Not, like, you know, in a dangerous way, he throws Egg Head an uneasy glance, "or, well, we're not going to do it in a dangerous way."
Zh: “不是，比如说，你也知道的，比较危险的方法，”他不安地瞥了一眼爱凡客，“或者，那个，我们会用不怎么危险的方法。”

En: Yes, chemistry has many uses. In police work, too, the lieutenant says, deadpan.
Zh: “是的，化学有很多用途。警察的工作也是，”警督面无表情地开了口。

En: The lead programmer turns to look at you for a moment, her gaze impassive, then returns to her work.
Zh: 主程序员回头看了你一会儿，目光冷漠，随后又转头继续工作。

En: I hope you get to experience one of our light-smell shows sometime, sir, he says with a smile, nodding his blond, spiked head vigorously. "It'll be a blast."
Zh: “希望你有空能来体验一下我们的声光秀，长官。”他微笑着，用力点着那颗长满金发的尖头。“一定会很震撼的。”

En: Feel free to come back later and 'have a blast' with these exemplars of youthful avant-gardism. But we really need to return to the present now.
Zh: “你之后随时可以回来，跟这帮年轻的先锋派先驱一起感受*震撼*。不过现在我们真的应该回归当下了。”

En: Nah, that's just the smell of healthy experimentation. And lots of chemicals.
Zh: 不，那只是健康实验的味道。还有很多化学物质的味道。

En: The young man looks startled for a moment, but then flashes you his most winsome smile.
Zh: 那个年轻人惊讶了一会儿，但是很快朝你露出了最迷人的微笑。

En: I hope you get to experience one of our light-smell shows sometime, sir, he says, nodding his blond, spiked head vigorously. "It'll be a blast."
Zh: “希望你有空能来体验一下我们的声光秀，长官。”他微笑着，用力点着那颗长满金发的尖头。“一定会很震撼的。”

En: I hope I did stuff like this when I was a kid.
Zh: 要是我的孩提时代也做过这种事就好了。

En: I'm sure you did. You have an exploratory, creative streak -- just like them.
Zh: 我肯定你做过。你拥有充满探究精神、富有创造力的性格——就像他们一样。

En: I'm not sure I'm buying what you're selling. What kind of effects?
Zh: “我不确定吃不吃你这套。是什么样的效果？”

En: Okay, sounds cool. (Nod along.)
Zh: “好吧，听起来挺牛掰的。”（点点头。）

En: Besides, did you know that *smell experiences* are all the rage now at the most forward-thinking venues?
Zh: “再说了，你知道现在*气味体验*在最前卫的地方火的一塌糊涂吗？”

En: Very big in *art circles*. She looks up for a moment.
Zh: 她抬头看了一眼。“在*艺术圈*火爆了。”

En: Hey! Her Innocence Dolores Dei liked little figurines, right? Liked holding little men between her fingers, remember?
Zh: 哈！无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛喜欢小雕像，对吗？喜欢把小人夹在她得手指之间，还记得吗？

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: No, I don't remember anything, I don't want to think about this. Good bye. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 没，我什么也不记得。我不想考虑这件事。再见。[放弃思维。]

En: You have the Headless FALN Rider figurine. You should give it to her. Win her back!
Zh: 你手上有无头FALN骑士雕像啊。你应该送给她。把她的心赢回来！

En: You have the revolutionary soldier figurine. You should give it to her. Win her back!
Zh: 你手上有革命战士雕像啊。你应该送给她。把她的心赢回来！

En: No... it would have been *beautiful*. Really tiny. Thoughtful. Clever!
Zh: 不……它应该是*美丽的*。非常小巧。体贴。聪明！

En: Whatever this was, it feels like you made the right choice not to peek into it.
Zh: 不管是什么，看来没有深入窥探是个正确决定。

En: What? (Look around.) Win who back? I can't win her back, she's a long dead historical figure.
Zh: 什么？（环顾四周。）把谁赢回来？我不能把她赢回来，她可是个早就已经去世的历史人物。

En: I should yes. This is a TASK of mine now.
Zh: 我应该回答说是。现在这是我的任务了。

En: Forget about this... [Discard thought.]
Zh: 还是忘了吧……[放弃思维。]

En: Don't be so pessimistic. Love doesn't die that easily!
Zh: 别这么悲观。爱情是不会轻易磨灭的！

En: This isn't funny... you have a bad feeling about this one.
Zh: 这不好笑……你感觉不太好。

En: So very, very, *very* nifty... nifty and mysterious. This is surely what the figurines are for!
Zh: 真是那么，那么，*那么*的漂亮……俏皮又神秘。雕像肯定就是这么用的！
